title: Chewy Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Chewy delivered strong Q4 2024 results, with net sales reaching $3.25 billion (15% YoY growth) and full-year 2024 sales of $11.86 billion (6% YoY growth). The company achieved adjusted EBITDA margin of 4.8% for the year, representing 150 basis points of expansion. A major milestone was the return to year-over-year active customer growth after eight quarters of declines. The company ended 2024 with 20.5 million active customers, a 2.1% increase. Management attributed this to improvements across all customer acquisition metrics—new customers, reactivations, and reduced churn. Autoship customer sales grew 21% in Q4 and 10.6% for the full year, significantly outpacing overall growth. This subscription-like revenue stream represented 80.6% of Q4 sales, demonstrating strong customer loyalty across consumables and health and wellness categories. The ads business reached approximately 1% of net sales in 2024, with management expecting the upper limit of long-term entitlement for sponsored ads business is up to 3% of total enterprise net sales over time. Eight vet care clinics opened in 2024 (upper end of guidance), with plans for 8-10 additional openings in 2025. The company generated record free cash flow of $452.5 million in 2024, converting approximately 80% of adjusted EBITDA. Chewy returned $943 million to shareholders through share repurchases, including buying back 29.4 million shares from largest shareholder BC Partners. Despite a normalizing pet industry (expected growth of 2.5-4.5%), Chewy expects to grow at 6-7%, reflecting continued market share capture. Management guided for 6-7% net sales growth, active customer growth in the low single-digit range, and adjusted EBITDA margin of 5.4-5.7% for 2025.
